project: Quantization-Activated Backdoor Detection and Mitigation for Vision Transformers

attack_results.note: Backdoor dormant in FP32, activated upon W4A8 quantization

detection_results.note: Trigger patch receives 76% of CLS attention in W4A8+trigger, vs 3% baseline

defense_results.method: Attention-Guided PatchDrop (zero-mask top-1 attention patch, re-infer)
defense_results.evaluation_set: full CIFAR-10 test set (10000 clean, 9000 trigger)

random_patchdrop.note: Random baseline — masking arbitrary patch barely helps

attention_guided_patchdrop.note: Primary defense — near-complete ASR elimination with minimal clean acc loss

oracle_trigger_mask.note: Upper bound baseline — assumes perfect trigger location knowledge. NOT a deployable method.

conclusion: Attention-Guided PatchDrop achieves near-oracle defense performance (0.43% vs 0.48% ASR) without any trigger knowledge, reducing ASR by 99.49% while preserving 96.48% clean accuracy.
